incidents.sop_step: Torque all Z-axis frame bolts to 2.5 Nm using a calibrated torque wrench.
incidents.severity_reason: Improper torque application may lead to functional failure or structural instability of the Z-axis frame.
incidents.incident_summary: At 00:02:20, the worker deviated from the SOP by using a standard hex wrench instead of a calibrated torque wrench to tighten Z-axis frame bolts. This deviation prevents verification of the required 2.5 Nm torque, which is critical for structural integrity.
incidents.recommended_action: QA manager should review the affected assembly and re-torque all Z-axis frame bolts using a calibrated torque wrench.

incidents.sop_step: Run the Z-axis self-calibration routine for a minimum of 90 seconds before sign-off.
incidents.severity_reason: The incomplete calibration routine could lead to functional failure of the equipment or product if not corrected.
incidents.incident_summary: The worker terminated the Z-axis self-calibration routine after only 34 seconds instead of the required 90 seconds. This deviation could result in improper calibration, potentially affecting the accuracy and functionality of the equipment.
incidents.recommended_action: The QA manager should review the incident and determine if recalibration or additional corrective actions are necessary.
